evidence: R D expenses were $13.8 billion in 2025, $16.5 billion in 2024 and $16.0 billion in 2023. In 2025, our R D investments focused on AI integration, heterogeneous computing architectures and next-generation process technologies
capital investments classified as construction in progress totaled $34.5 billion ($50.4 billion as of December 28, 2024)

we entered into an agreement with NVIDIA to issue and sell to NVIDIA 215 million shares...for an aggregate cash purchase price of $5.0 billion...to support our strategic investments in advanced manufacturing, AI infrastructure and long-term growth initiatives
we announced our development of Crescent Island, our first GPU to be built on the advanced Xe3P architecture...engineered specifically for data center environments...for AI inference workloads
we introduced an open software platform, Open Edge, designed to make it easier for businesses to run AI and other applications at the edge

We released our initial Intel Core Ultra Series 3 processors, the first products to be manufactured using our new Intel 18A process technology...offer further improved CPU, AI and graphics performance
the evolving market for products with AI capabilities
our competitive environment has intensified in recent years...including as a result of the proliferation of AI and high demand for AI-related products and services
competitors include...accelerator products such as GPUs, including th[ose from NVIDIA]...many of our competitors have substantially greater financial resources than us

limitations: R&D figures are total company R&D, not AI-specific; no explicit AI-only capex dollar amount is broken out, reducing precision on investment intensity score
Construction-in-progress figure covers all manufacturing nodes, not exclusively AI-related; multi-year AI-specific capex plan not explicitly quantified, limiting score to 4 rather than 5

Risk section excerpts appear partial/truncated; risk disclosure score based on available risk-adjacent language in business description sections rather than a complete risk factors section, which may understate actual disclosure depth
Several AI product descriptions (Crescent Island, Jaguar Shores) are forward-looking announcements rather than confirmed deployed production systems, which tempers concreteness confidence
